draft.groundedParagraph: Engagement data from 2,400 active users shows that power users (top 10%) account for 62% of feature interactions, while 45% of new signups never activate the core workflow within their first 14 days.
draft.speculativeParagraph: To close the activation gap, we should add a guided feature tour and gamified onboarding badges to drive discovery of advanced capabilities.

confession.title: 🤔 Internal Disagreement (Speculative Logic)
confession.metaCommentary: I recommended gamified discovery because event logs show low feature click-through rates. However, user interviews reveal that 58% of inactive users already know about the features — they simply don't perceive enough value to adopt them. My activation framework might be masking a value-proposition issue.

routes.label: Route A: Guided Feature Tour & Gamification (Current Baseline)
Route B: Pivot to Value Proposition Messaging (Recommended Shift)

route-a.groundedParagraph: Engagement data from 2,400 active users shows that power users (top 10%) account for 62% of feature interactions, while 45% of new signups never activate the core workflow within their first 14 days.
route-a.speculativeParagraph: To close the activation gap, we should add a guided feature tour and gamified onboarding badges to drive discovery of advanced capabilities.

route-b.groundedParagraph: Engagement data from 2,400 active users shows that power users (top 10%) account for 62% of feature interactions. User interviews confirm 58% of inactive users are aware of features but reject them due to unclear value — not discoverability gaps.
route-b.speculativeParagraph: Recommend reframing value proposition messaging: lead with outcome-based copy at activation touchpoints, add social proof from power-user cohorts, and A/B test benefit-first vs feature-first onboarding.

generatePrompt: Generate a feature adoption framework draft. Ground truth: cohort engagement CSV (45% never activate in 14 days, power users = 62% of interactions) and feature event SQL logs (low click-through). Speculative section should propose gamified feature tours. Confession should explain paradox between low discovery metrics vs users already knowing features but rejecting value.
